title: Ceropegia stephanotis
taxonomy: Ceropegia stephanotis is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It belongs to the genus Ceropegia, which includes about 100 species of flowering plants. The genus Ceropegia is part of the order Gentianales, a group of flowering plants that also includes the families Apocynaceae, Asclepiadaceae, and Loganiaceae. The plant's classification places it within the larger clade of asterids, a group of flowering plants that also includes the families Lamiaceae and Solanaceae. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships between Ceropegia stephanotis and other flowering plants.

intro: Ceropegia stephanotis, also known as the "Wire Vine" or "Stephanotis Ceropegia", is a flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family, native to the tropical regions of Asia. Traditionally, this plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The bioactive constituents of Ceropegia stephanotis include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional medicine, Ceropegia stephanotis is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as being used in herbal teas and tinctures, to treat its associated health benefits.
